title: Change Your Brain, Change Your Life
author: Daniel G. Amen

chapters.title: Your brain controls everything you think, feel and do — look after it!
chapters.text:
Before you embark on any treatment plan, be it a diet or anything else, you need to know your starting point. That gives you information to work with and helps you to track your progress. When it comes to brain health, specific measurements will tell you what you need to change.

The first to consider is your weight. Sure, you can get on a set of scales and read the numbers, but that doesn’t give you a complete picture. Instead, look towards your Body Mass Index or BMI. You can easily find a BMI calculator online to provide you with your specific number. A high BMI gives you a greater chance of reduced brain activity through less tissue in the brain. Over time, this increases your chance of age-related degenerative diseases and depression.

A healthy BMI is 18.5-25. Anything over that could increase your chances of poor brain function.

Another critical measurement is the ratio between your waist and height. When you have a high waist-to-height ratio, you carry more abdominal fat. This is a more dangerous type of fat because it produces hormones that can increase your chances of developing diabetes. Unbalanced hormones can also harm the brain.

Your blood pressure, or BP, is a crucial measurement that you need to take and monitor regularly. This can be done at your doctor’s surgery or home if you have a machine. High blood pressure, or hypertension, has been linked with low cognitive function and poor memory and decision-making.

Finally, it would help if you focused on how much sleep you’re getting at night. Sleep doesn’t mean you’re lazy; it means you’re looking after your brain! You need to aim for between 7 to 8 hours of sleep every night. The less sleep you get over time, the less functional your brain becomes.

Sleep deprivation is a serious issue. Not only is your focus and ability to make decisions affected, but your metabolism and your hormonal balance too.

chapters.title: To improve your cognitive function, you first need to understand your brain
chapters.text:
Before you begin to soothe your brain through the suggestions posed throughout the summary, you need to understand how the brain works. After all, you can’t be genuinely convinced of the efficiency of something if you have no idea why it’s going to be beneficial!

Your brain is responsible for everything you do throughout the course of the day, and while you’re sleeping, it clears out all the toxins that, if allowed to build up, would make you sick over time. The brain only weighs around three pounds, and it is kept safe inside your skull. The only problem is that your skull is full of sharp points and edges, so it’s much easier to damage your brain than you might realize.

The brain is an integrated whole, a symphony of parts that work together to create and sustain a life. ~ Daniel G. Amen, MD

While the brain is composed of more parts than we can mention, there are several vital elements to bear in mind:
• The cerebral cortex is the most significant part of the brain and comprises the temporal, frontal, occipital, and parietal lobes. This part of your brain controls direction, vision, focus, memory, learning, mood stability, and processing sounds and pictures.
• The limbic system is a deeper structure in the brain. This is your emotional system, where emotions are formed and interpreted.
• The basal ganglia is another structure located close to the limbic system, which controls pleasure, motivation, and movement.
• The cerebellum at the back of the brain is where your thoughts are coordinated and your motor skills controlled.
• Your brain is divided into a left and right side, with one being more dominant than the other.

Have you ever wondered why smells make you feel a certain way? That’s because your sense of smell is linked to your limbic system, which attaches deeply emotional memories to smells.

All parts of your brain work together to process information. This chain of events happens almost instantly. If your brain is working sluggishly, this could be because of a health issue within your body, affecting your brain function.

Did you know? You are either right-brain dominant or left-brain dominant. Left-brained people are more analytical, while right-brained people tend to be more creative.

chapters.title: Learn to be focused on the health of your brain to improve your whole health picture
chapters.text:
Daniel Amen suggests that specific ailments can result from an unhealthy brain and that by taking actions to soothe the part of the brain the condition is connected with, you right all wrongs.

We know that the brain is the control center for everything you feel, see, do, and interpret. When your brain is happy and healthy, you’re less troubled by upsetting issues, such as anxiety or depression, and have a more comfortable and successful picture in front of you.

Anxiety and depression are rife in the modern world, but it’s not always external factors that cause them; sometimes, the cause can be your lifestyle and the people you surround yourself with.

However, few people know how to look after their brains. This sophisticated organ might only be tiny compared to the rest of your body, but it takes up a massive 30% of your daily intake of energy from the calories you consume. It needs this energy to do all the amazing things it does daily. However, the brain’s consistency is very soft, and even though you have a skull protecting it, you need to be very careful that you don’t accidentally bump your head. Even a minor head injury can cause damage to your brain in some way.

It’s not just that your brain is soft that puts it at risk; it also comes down to the things you do with your life. If you drink too much alcohol or do drugs, you’re going to affect your brain’s function. If you have a poor diet and become obese, you’re going to affect how your brain works. Countless things can detrimentally affect your brain, including unbalanced hormones, high blood pressure, and even thinking negatively.

It can be worrying that so many things can damage your brain’s health and function, but you can make many positive changes to alter that picture. Focus on learning new things, eating the right foods, and surrounding yourself with positive, happy people.

Omega-3 fatty acids in fish and B vitamins can help you boost your brain health through your diet.

Looking at treatment through a body, mind, social, and spiritual approach covers all bases and gives you the best chance at changing your brain health and your life.

chapters.title: Know your magic numbers to track your progress
chapters.text:
Before you embark on any treatment plan, be it a diet or anything else, you need to know your starting point. That gives you information to work with and helps you to track your progress. When it comes to brain health, specific measurements will tell you what you need to change.

chapters.title: Balance your emotions by healing your limbic system
chapters.text:
A vital part of the brain is the limbic system — the control center for emotions. When something happens, you remember or hear something; the limbic system decides how you interpret it. Will you be angry about it, sad or happy? In addition to being your emotional center, the limbic system is also a motivational part that also helps to regulate sleep and appetite.

A bad mood has the power to ruin your day and cause you to be excessively pessimistic. Soothe your limbic system for a more positive attitude and a more optimistic outlook.

When you have a problem in this part of your brain, i.e., it’s working too hard, you may notice several issues arising. The main ones are:
• Depression and general sadness
• Thinking negatively
• Constantly feeling negative emotions
• Trouble sleeping
• Always feeling hungry or never feeling hungry
• Chronic pain
• Isolating yourself from those around you for no specific reason

Identifying these issues means you need to put a plan into action to soothe your limbic system. Over a short amount of time, you should notice that your problems start to ease. Of course, for depression, your doctor may prescribe antidepressant medication or specific supplements, but you can also try holistic therapies, such as acupuncture or aromatherapy. Transcranial magnetic stimulation therapy is sometimes beneficial for limbic system problems or neurofeedback. However, getting enough exercise could be all it takes to fix this part of your brain.

Aim to get up and move around more every single day. Exercise is a powerful tool for not only helping to regulate your weight but boost your mood too.

Daniel Amen suggests that you also try keeping a gratitude diary and appreciating the things and people around you more. Surround yourself with people who you enjoy spending time with and who are positive. Small acts of kindness are another valuable tool in this regard. All of this can help the limbic system become healthier.

chapters.title: Regular anxiety could mean your basal ganglia need some attention
chapters.text:
If the limbic system is all about emotions, the basal ganglia is about anxiety and unnecessary fear. When you are regularly anxious, you find it hard to control your anxiety, or you’re always living in fear. It could be that your basal ganglia is working too hard and needs a little care and attention.

This part of the brain helps to coordinate your fine motor skills with how you feel. It also helps with fine motor skills and movements. In addition, this part of the brain is responsible for keeping anxiety at bay, forming habits, and balancing motivation and pleasure.

When you have a problem with the basal ganglia, you may notice:
• Feeling overly negative or nervous
• Anxiety, i.e., a racing mind and physical symptoms of anxiety, such as panic attacks
• Feelings of doom and always thinking the worst
• Avoiding any argument or conflict
• Avoiding taking any risk
• Tension in the muscles
• Problems with motor skills
• Lack of motivation

Many doctors will prescribe medication for severe anxiety, but there are other non-medical options you can try too. These actions will all help to balance and soothe the basal ganglia. For instance, you can try using your breath to control your thoughts. This is called diaphragmatic breathing and allows you to slow your breathing down and make sure that you’re taking your breath from the diaphragm and not breathing shallowly from the chest.

Using breath to calm and ground you in troubling times is a good tactic. Place your hand on your abdomen and direct the breath from there. Slow things down, and be mindful of every breath you take.

Ensuring that you’re getting a well-varied diet is also crucial in soothing every area of your brain. Get plenty of D vitamins and focus on getting enough oily fish into your diet, as this is packed with omega-3 fatty acids, perfect for brain health and function. It’s a good idea to eat small, regular meals throughout the day, keeping your blood sugar levels on an even keel. Low blood sugar has been shown to worsen anxiety problems and even contribute to the physical sensations of a panic attack.

The food you eat has an important effect on how you feel and your levels of stress anxiety. ~ Daniel G. Amen, MD

Many people find that hypnosis is a good tool for calming this brain area and avoiding racing thoughts and anxiety. This can be done with a professional, or you can try self-hypnosis if you prefer. Guided imagery and meditation are two other options you can try.

chapters.title: Soothe your prefrontal cortex for better organizational skills
chapters.text:
Many people struggle with time management, poor organizational skills, impulsive actions, and difficulty focusing and paying attention. This can sometimes be due to simply being in the setting while trying to work, but it can also be a problem with the prefrontal cortex.

Don’t necessarily assume that you have a problem with your prefrontal cortex if you struggle with focus for a short period. Stress comes and goes in life. However, if this is a prolonged issue, soothing the prefrontal cortex can help.

The prefrontal cortex is responsible for impulse control, focus and concentration, and judgment. It also affects your ability to solve problems and come up with creative solutions. Your ability to express emotion and make plans is also affected by your prefrontal cortex. Those who struggle with Attention Deficit Disorder (ADD) have an overactivity or under-activity in the prefrontal cortex.

When this part of the brain is sluggish or working too hard, the following issues can occur:
• A short attention span
• Being easily distracted
• Giving in at the first sign of struggle
• Not being able to control emotions well
• Not being able to control impulses well
• Hyperactivity
• Problems with time management
• A tendency to procrastinate
• Being disorganized much of the time

Exercise is a handy way to soothe this part of the brain, but in some cases, medications may be required, e.g., for those diagnosed with ADD and require extra help. Daniel Amen suggests you try meditation if you struggle with any of the problems mentioned above. This can help you be more aware of what is going on around you and build your concentration skills.

Using time management interventions, such as working in timed sections, setting small goals, and breaking tasks down into milestones, can also help with focus and avoiding procrastination. To try and overcome impulsive actions, looking for excitement in the right places is essential. This means questioning every action before taking it and thinking of the bigger picture to make the right choices.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Much of how the brain works is still a mystery. This organ has unexplored depths, and while we know the basics of what goes on, there is still much to learn. We all have this sophisticated and wonderful organ inside our skulls, so remember to love yours! It is a part of you, and it controls everything about who you are and what you feel and do.

The fact that the brain is soft and easily damaged adds to its preciousness. We have to look after our brains if we want to feel better in life, avoid common problems, and be happier overall. Overeating, not exercising enough, doing drugs, drinking too much alcohol, and being too negative can all affect how well your brain works. It’s easy to say, “don’t do any of those things,” but it’s not always that simple. It’s often about taking baby steps towards changing ingrained habits that have been part of you for a long time. However, awareness is an essential step towards making changes.

If you spend a lot of time looking after how your body appears, why not start turning that attention inwards instead. Having a rounded approach to health is the best way to go. That doesn’t only mean thinking about problems at the source and dealing with them as they arise; it means looking beyond and identifying why issues might be occurring and where they come from. Most of the time, the issue originates within the brain and points towards an imbalance or an over-or under-activity that needs to be addressed.

The good news is that looking after your brain doesn’t involve complicated therapies and treatments. Simply focusing on being healthier and looking beyond the physical is all it takes.

Try this


• Start a gratitude diary. Every day, write down one thing you’re grateful for on that particular day. Review it every week and see how fortunate you are.
• Try to exercise for at least half an hour every day. This can be going for a walk in the evening or going for a swim; whatever works for you!
• Aim to have at least one portion of oily fish in your diet every week. Try salmon, sardines, trout, and mackerel to get you started.

Read it till the end?
